author: @OverUnity7734
text: 6:16 Because the speed of magnatizm is the same as the universal constant, you will not get any more energy out of it when the magnets move faster than the speed of light.

author: @thomasseigler8875
text: Nice... is there such a thing as " too fast" ? Limited only by the imagination of the creator of the divice. There's no such thing as a box for Robert. He'll never stay inside one when coming up with these ideas. Lol. Have a great one.

author: @ASMRPoohbear
text: Got to admit, while I find 3d printing, I’m like many an old school engineer and like making things out of trad materials with hands and tinkering with mechanics. This channel was interesting before printing took over as a necessity for every video. Decided to unsubscribe because you’ve drifted away from the everyday tickering mechanic and engineer that has a shed. 😢

replies.author: @waynefergusson9987
replies.text: Thats a strange perception as the details are the same

replies.author: @jerbear7952
replies.text: I have to admit I felt the same way at first. Then I thought about my welder. I put off learning to weld for years becaused I was a bit afraid it would be too challenging to learn. Boy am I glad I stepped my game up and added welding to my skills. I just added laser engraving and 3d printing is next. Either way if Robert isn't scratching that itch anymore, I can't blame you. It sure would be worth checking in every once in a while though. He's going to use a cadbury cup and some flour paste to solve fusion any video now.

author: @joshuafoster8976
text: Would variable gearing be better 2cones and a belt..... that way your turbine wouldnt over rotate in high wind🤔🤔🤔

author: @jima3252
text: Using a hairdryer for wind on a converted PC brushless fan motor as described in video 1959 I was able to get around 17 volts open circuit, and around .3 amps shorted. When bridge rectified and hooked to an led chip with 15 LEDs in parallel, and no resistor, it lit up very bright. With some gearing a simple savonius type rotor would probably do the trick. The daisy idea seems very good btw. Keep up the good work.

author: @travismoore7849
text: Those hand crank generators work with a diode bridge to send the output to a battery or super capacitor.

replies.author: @OverUnity7734
replies.text: Loseing energy in the diodes and the battery / capacitor, is not my idea of "best".

replies.author: @travismoore7849
replies.text: @OverUnity7734 Of curse they loose energy but how do you not use a blocking diode to keep the energy from discharging through the coils?

replies.author: @travismoore7849
replies.text: I guess you use the LED as a blocking diode in series with the battery and coils?
